title: Abstract Hook Configuration
description: Platform-agnostic hook definition for multi-agent hook support. Emitters translate this into platform-specific configs (Claude Code settings.json, Codex hooks.json, Pi settings.json, Gemini settings.json, OpenCode plugin).

hooks.description: Hook definitions keyed by event name. Each key is an event name containing an array of hook groups.

additionalProperties.description: Unknown event names are not allowed. Use only the defined event names.

hookGroups.description: Array of hook groups. Each group has an optional matcher and one or more hooks.

matcher.description: Regex or pipe-separated tool/event names. Empty string or '*' matches all. For tool events, matches against tool_name. For session events, matches against source/reason.

hooks.description: Array of hooks to execute when the matcher matches.

type.description: Hook type. 'command' executes a shell command. 'prompt' uses an LLM for evaluation (Claude Code only).

command.description: Shell command to execute. Required when type='command'. Use $PROJECT_DIR and $PLUGIN_ROOT as abstract path placeholders — emitters replace them with platform-specific env vars.

prompt.description: Natural language prompt for LLM evaluation. Required when type='prompt'. Only supported on Claude Code; other platforms emit a warning and skip.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. Default: 600 for command hooks, 60 for prompt hooks. Platform-specific defaults apply if omitted.

if.description: Optional condition in ToolName(pattern) syntax, e.g. 'Bash(git push*)'. Only supported on Pi (pi-hooks). Other platforms ignore this field with a warning.
